data.title: Two-day seminar on U.S. power industry, renewable energy, ISO markets, and power transactions in Charlotte, NC on Oct 7-8, 2025.
data.description: A training course titled "Today's U.S. Electric Power Industry, Renewable Energy, ISO Markets, and Power Transactions" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's portfolio. The course will take place in Charlotte, NC, United States on October 7-8, 2025. It aims to enhance understanding of the U.S. electric power sector and the various mechanisms involved in power transactions. The seminar offers a detailed overview of the industry's structure, operations, and current trends. This comprehensive program is ideal for individuals seeking insights into the U.S. electric power industry landscape.

data.title: KOIL Energy Solutions awarded a significant contract for subsea isolation valve control equipment to protect offshore platforms.
data.description: On August 29, 2025, Koil Energy Solutions, Inc. (OTCQB: KLNG), a global expert in subsea technology, revealed the acquisition of a noteworthy contract. This deal involves the provision of control equipment for a subsea isolation valve system designed to safeguard offshore platforms and workers from the accidental discharge of hydrocarbons from subsea wells. The contract involves the full scope of engineering, procurement, and production of KOIL's reliable and established technical system for Subsea Safety Control Systems. This development marks a significant step for Koil Energy Solutions, Inc. in the specialized sector of subsea equipment and services.

data.title: OM price plunges 6.6% to $0.22 amid bearish pressure, eyes $0.21 support. Technicals signal further downside.
data.description: The price of OM token has fallen by 6.59% to $0.22, as per recent trading data. Technical indicators suggest bearish momentum for the token as it nears crucial support levels.

data.title: Refresco acquires Norway's Telemark Kildevann, a top beverage maker, in latest expansion move.
data.description: Press Release: Refresco has finalized the acquisition of Telemark Kildevann, a prominent beverage manufacturer based in Norway. The deal involves a substantial exchange of assets, estimated to be worth several million dollars. This acquisition is set to significantly expand Refresco's presence in the Norwegian market and strengthen its position as a key player in the beverage industry.

data.title: Stock market erases gains as Alibaba news hits Nvidia, AI plays, with the S&P 500 hitting record highs before major indexes wiping out weekly gains.
data.description: The S&P 500 achieved unprecedented highs, however, major indexes forfeited their weekly gains due to a report surrounding Alibaba which compounded worries related to Nvidia's operations in China. This development came amidst a broader context of the market responding to news regarding AI plays. The post on Investor's Business Daily highlighted how these factors led to the erasure of gains in the stock market, underlining the impact of the Alibaba news on Nvidia and the overarching role of AI developments during the week.

data.title: AI agents require intent-driven blockchain setup to prevent recurring Web3 issues; devs must make this integration for success.
data.description: It is crucial for developers to incorporate AI agents into their systems focusing on intent to avoid repeating the issues that Web3 aimed to solve initially. Failure to do so may lead to setbacks in achieving the objectives set forth by Web3 technologies.
Announcement of THEON's Capital Markets Day "THEON NEXT" event showcasing future plans and strategies.

data.title: CVS & Walgreens tighten Covid vaccine rules in several states due to no CDC panel recommendation; prescriptions needed.
data.description: The largest pharmacy chains in the country are currently not providing COVID-19 booster shots without prescriptions or are refraining from administering them due to the absence of a recommendation from a C.D.C. panel. This decision is in accordance with certain state laws.

data.title: French economy shows marginal GDP growth of 0.3% in Q2 2025, amid political crisis over debt; Macron's Cabinet faces collapse.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, the French economy experienced a modest growth of 0.3%, following a 0.1% increase in the previous quarter. However, there was a 0.3% decline in net foreign trade. Personal spending in France dropped by 0.30% in July, leading to a decrease in consumer confidence to 87 in August (below expectations of 90). The government, under President Emmanuel Macron, faced a political crisis regarding its debt, prompting Prime Minister Francois Bayrou to call for a confidence vote. Amidst fears of a possible bailout from the IMF, economist Michael A. Arouet warned of a potential second "Euro crisis" originating in France. French Finance Minister Eric Lombard acknowledged the risk of an IMF intervention and expressed the government's intention to avoid such a scenario. The political instability has led to calls for new national elections in France, reflecting deep dissatisfaction with the current status quo.
Proactis SA reports half-year revenue for July 2025, showing significant growth in numbers.

data.title: Netflix stock up 38% YTD, "KPop Demon Hunters" earns $18M, first box office No.1, with 1,150 sold-out showings.
data.description: Netflix stock experienced a positive trend on Friday, showcasing its value through a diverse content slate, live sports ventures, password regulation, and price increases. The stock has increased by more than 38% since the beginning of the year. Noteworthy events at Netflix this week include the streaming service achieving its first-ever top box office ranking with the film "KPop Demon Hunters," which indicates a significant change in its movie strategy. Partnering with Regal and Cinemark, Netflix organized a two-day theatrical release for the animated musical, generating approximately $18 million in revenue despite being accessible for streaming since June. The company utilized exclusive screenings, a sing-along option, and solid merchandising tactics to generate hype, resulting in over 1,150 sold-out screenings.
